topic: The Schumann Math Error — Section 6.3 review
our_argument_summary: Section 6.3 claims the dome's stated 5.3% error between H_Schumann (9,572 km) and the model value is wrong, asserting that the dome compares to H(r=0) = 8,537 km, which yields 10.8% or 12.1% — not 5.3%. Section 6.3 concludes: 'The page's own numbers contradict its stated error.' It then adds that the dome silently switches to the globe Schumann formula f = c/(2πR) to get 7.83 Hz.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No code pipeline for this section — it's about a mathematical claim on the dome's website, not monitoring code.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — section-level review, not WIN-level code analysis.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review; code analysis tags apply to WIN-029 (already reviewed as hardcoded).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The Schumann resonance is standard physics since Schumann (1952) / Balser & Wagner (1960).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The dome back-calculates height from the known 7.83 Hz measured value.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: H = c/(4f) is an inversion of the measured frequency, not a forward prediction from dome geometry.

holes_found.description: Section 6.3 MISIDENTIFIES the dome's comparison target, making our math-error accusation factually incorrect. The dome's evolution page (06-evolution.txt, line 68) explicitly states: 'Matches model H = 9,086 km to 5.3%.' The dome is comparing H_Schumann = 9,572 km to the stated firmament_height parameter of 9,086 km — NOT to H(r=0) = 8,537 km. Computed: (9572 - 9086) / 9086 = 5.35%, which rounds to 5.3%. Our section claims 'Model H(r=0) = 8,537 km' is the comparison point and concludes 'The page's own numbers contradict its stated error.' This accusation is wrong. The 5.3% is arithmetically correct for the value the dome actually cites. A dome defender can immediately refute this paragraph by pointing to the evolution page and the 9,086 km comparison, making our review look sloppy — exactly the credibility damage we cannot afford.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite Section 6.3 to acknowledge that the 5.3% arithmetic is correct when comparing to firmament_height = 9,086 km, then pivot to the REAL problem: the dome has two internally inconsistent firmament heights (8,537 km from H(r=0) and 9,086 km from the model parameterization), and 9,086 km is mathematically impossible under the dome's own H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) which maxes out at 8,537 km at r=0. Section 2.1 already makes this argument devastatingly. Section 6.3 should lead with: 'The 5.3% error claim uses the dome's stated firmament_height of 9,086 km — but this value exceeds the maximum of the dome's own H(r) curve (8,537 km at r=0). The dome's firmament height formula contradicts its firmament height parameter. Using the actual H(r=0) = 8,537 km gives 10.8–12.1%, more than double the claimed error. The dome cannot have both 9,086 km and H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619); the 5.3% claim depends on the incoherent value.'

holes_found.description: The second paragraph of Section 6.3 ('the dome's parallel-plate cavity geometry actually predicts Schumann frequencies around 22 Hz') is correct and well-supported by Section 2.1, but 'parallel-plate' is slightly imprecise. The dome's geometry is an exponentially-varying curved shell over a disc, not parallel plates. Section 2.1 correctly describes this as 'two parallel circular plates (upper dome, lower sump)' which is a simplification for the quarter-wave analysis. Section 6.3 should use the same framing as Section 2.1 for consistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Minor wording: change 'parallel-plate cavity geometry' to 'exponential cavity geometry' to match Section 2.1's framing and the dome's actual geometric claim.

holes_found.description: Section 6.3 is very short (2 paragraphs, ~160 words). Given that this is a section within Part 6 (Predictions Page — Structural Analysis), it should be more self-contained. Currently it relies entirely on a cross-reference to Section 2.1 for the ~22 Hz argument without summarizing why. A reader scanning Part 6 alone (without clicking to Part 2) gets only the math-error argument (which is currently wrong) and a bare assertion about 22 Hz.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand slightly to include the key argument inline: the dome's quarter-wave formula f=c/(4H) gives 8.8 Hz at the pole and ~59 Hz at the equator; the area-weighted average gives ~22 Hz; the observed 7.83 Hz is recovered only by the globe's spherical-shell formula f = c√(n(n+1))/(2πR). This makes Section 6.3 self-sufficient without bloating it.

stronger_arguments.description: The Section 6.3 rewrite should emphasize the TRIPLE inconsistency: (1) the dome claims firmament_height = 9,086 km; (2) the dome's own H(r) formula gives H(0) = 8,537 km; (3) the dome needs 9,572 km to match Schumann. None of these three numbers agree with each other. The 5.3% between 9,572 and 9,086 is the smallest gap, but neither 9,572 nor 9,086 is derivable from the dome's own exponential formula. This triple inconsistency is stronger than just calling it a 'math error' — it reveals a deeper structural incoherence where the model has three incompatible height values with no acknowledged reconciliation.
sources: raw-text/05-model.txt line 21: 'Model parameterization (9,086 km) = near-pole average'
raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 68: 'Matches model H = 9,086 km to 5.3%'

stronger_arguments.description: Section 2.1 already proves rigorously that 9,086 km cannot be a 'near-pole average' of H(r) since H(r) never exceeds 8,537 km. Section 6.3 should explicitly cite this impossibility proof rather than just cross-referencing Section 2.1 generically. The argument is: sup{H(r)} = 8,537 km, therefore E[H(r)] ≤ 8,537 km for any weighting, therefore 9,086 km as an 'average' is a mathematical impossibility. This kills the dome's own stated reconciliation.
sources: Section 2.1 text: 'E[f(X)] ≤ sup f(X) is an elementary theorem'

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that back-calculating a cavity height from measured Schumann frequency IS a legitimate scientific technique. In standard physics, inverting f₁ = c/(2πR)·√(2) for R gives a reasonable estimate of the Earth-ionosphere cavity radius. The idea that resonance frequencies encode cavity geometry is solid physics.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The dome applies the wrong formula (quarter-wave linear waveguide instead of spherical cavity eigenmode) and obtains a height (9,572 km) that doesn't match its own model by either parameter value (8,537 or 9,086 km). More critically, even if the formula and comparison were correct, the height derived from Schumann places the cavity at ~60-100 km (using the correct globe formula), not at 8,500-9,600 km. The dome's back-calculation produces a number 100× too large because it uses an inappropriate resonance formula for its geometry.

deeper_analysis:
This is a significant quality issue. Section 6.3's central accusation — that the dome's 5.3% doesn't match its own numbers — is factually wrong because we identified the wrong comparison target. The dome clearly states (evolution page) that it compares to 9,086 km, not 8,537 km. The arithmetic (9572-9086)/9086 = 5.35% ≈ 5.3% is correct.

However, the REAL argument is much stronger than what we currently have: the dome's 9,086 km parameter is itself a ghost number that cannot be derived from the dome's own H(r) curve. Section 2.1 already proves this with mathematical rigor (H(r) ≤ 8,537 for all r, so no average can exceed 8,537). The correct attack is not 'your arithmetic is wrong' but 'your arithmetic is correct on a number your own model cannot produce.'

This is a textbook case of attacking the wrong thing: we caught a surface-level error (mismatched comparison) when the deeper structural problem (the comparison target is itself incoherent) is far more devastating. The rewrite should:
1. Acknowledge the 5.3% is arithmetically correct
2. Pivot immediately to: but 9,086 km cannot exist under H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619)
3. Show the TRIPLE inconsistency: 9,572 ≠ 9,086 ≠ 8,537
4. Cross-reference Section 2.1's rigorous impossibility proof
5. Keep the second paragraph (globe formula switching) which is correct

Priority: HIGH. This is a credibility risk — a dome defender finding this error would undermine trust in the whole review. We criticize the dome for math errors; we cannot afford our own.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender would say: 'Your Section 6.3 accuses me of a math error, but the 5.3% is calculated from 9,086 km — my stated model parameterization — exactly as described on my evolution page. You substituted 8,537 km (from my H(r) curve at r=0) and then claimed my arithmetic is wrong. This is either careless reading or deliberate misrepresentation. If your review can't even identify which number I'm comparing to, why should anyone trust your analysis of anything else?' This defense is devastating because it's factually correct — we DID misidentify the comparison target.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Rewrite Section 6.3 immediately. The current text is indefensible against this critique. The rewrite must (a) correctly identify 9,086 km as the comparison target, (b) show the 5.3% arithmetic works, then (c) pivot to why 9,086 km itself is the problem — it exceeds the dome's own H(r) maximum. This transforms a factual error in our review into a much stronger argument.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No inconsistencies between Section 6.3 and the WIN-level reviews. WIN-029 review (Cycle 2) correctly identifies both firmament heights (8,537 and 9,086) and flags the 6.4% internal discrepancy. The inconsistency is between Section 6.3's prose (which picks only 8,537) and WIN-029's review (which correctly identifies both). The WIN-level analysis is right; the section prose is wrong.

quantitative_verification.computations: Python verification: c=299792, f=7.83 → H=c/(4f)=9571.9 km ✓. (9572-9086)/9086=5.35%≈5.3% ✓. (9572-8537)/9572=10.81%≈10.8% ✓. (9572-8537)/8537=12.13%≈12.1% ✓. All arithmetic in our Section 6.3 is correct — the error is in identifying WHICH model value the dome uses, not in the calculations themselves.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The calculations are all correct. The factual error is in the premise: our section assumes the dome compares to 8,537 km when it actually compares to 9,086 km. With the correct comparison target, the dome's 5.3% claim is valid.

citations_verified: Section 2.1 cross-reference — verified that Section 2.1 exists and contains the H(r) impossibility argument.
citations_unchecked: The globe Schumann formula f = c/(2πR) is correctly attributed as standard physics but no specific citation is given in Section 6.3.
